{% set terms_exist = doc.terms and (doc.terms|striptags).strip() or "" %} {% if letter_head and not no_letterhead -%}
{{ letter_head }}
{%- endif %}

Date: {{ doc.offer_date }}

Dear {{ doc.applicant_name }},

We are pleased to appoint you in the services of {{ doc.company }} on the terms and conditions detailed in this letter.

Your designation shall be {{ doc.designation }}.

{%- if doc.offer_terms -%} {%- for row in doc.offer_terms -%} {{ row.offer_term }}: {{ row.value }}
{%- endfor -%} {%- endif -%}
Please read the detailed terms as below. If you have any queries, feel free to get in touch with us. We look forward to your long and fruitful career association with our organisation. If you decide to join us, 'Welcome to {{ doc.company }} !'

Yours truly,



Authorized Signatory
{{ doc.company }}

{% if terms_exist %}
{{ doc.terms }}
{% endif %}

html:
MAVEN WORKFORCE
MAVEN WORKFORCE INC
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT AND COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE

This EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT AND COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE is entered into and made effective this {{ frappe.utils.formatdate(frappe.utils.nowdate()) }} by and between Maven Workforce INC, a Delaware Corporation hereinafter referred to as "EMPLOYER" and {{ doc.item_name }} hereinafter referred to as "EMPLOYEE".

WITNESSETH

WHEREAS, the EMPLOYER is engaged in the business of providing Human Capital related consulting and contract services to its customers and clients (“CLIENTS”);

WHEREAS, EMPLOYER recruits and employs individuals who are qualified to provide such services, and places such individuals where needed; and

WHEREAS, as an express condition of EMPLOYEE’s employment with EMPLOYER, or as an express condition to EMPLOYEE’s continued employment with EMPLOYER, EMPLOYEE is being required to agree to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, and EMPLOYEE desires to do so.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the parties’ mutual promises, and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. Employment:

EMPLOYER hereby agrees to employ EMPLOYEE, subject to EMPLOYEE’s agreement to the following terms and conditions.

2. Term:

Employment will commence after the Employer performs its background check and verifies the Employee’s documentation and, if applicable, obtains client approval for the placement of the Employee. The term of this Agreement shall be until notice of termination is given by either party to the other, as stated in Section 5.

3. Employee Services:

3.1.The EMPLOYEE shall, during the term hereof, perform, to the best of his/her ability, all technical and administrative duties of {{doc.custom_designation or ''}} of the EMPLOYER at CLIENT sites, which may be assigned to the EMPLOYEE from time to time by the EMPLOYER or EMPLOYER’s CLIENT.

3.2.The EMPLOYEE shall report and be directly accountable to the President or a designated representative of the EMPLOYER and he/she agrees to devote all of his/her business time and attention to the business and affairs of the EMPLOYER, to perform all the duties that may be required of him/her pursuant to the express and implied terms hereof to the satisfaction of the EMPLOYER, and to exert his/her best efforts in the performance of his/her duties hereunder, so as to promote the profit, benefit, business and advantage of EMPLOYER and to represent the EMPLOYER in the most professional manner possible.

3.3.The aforesaid duties and services shall be rendered by the EMPLOYEE at the EMPLOYER’s offices or any business location of any CLIENT of the EMPLOYER to which the EMPLOYEE may be assigned, from time to time, at the discretion of the EMPLOYER.

4. Compensation:

During the term of this Agreement, EMPLOYEE will receive compensation according to the terms set forth in the employment offer letter. All the appraisals or salary revisions will take place on or after the anniversary date. Exceptions can be made at the Management’s approval.

5. Termination:

The term of this Agreement is “at-will”, meaning that either party may terminate for any reason or no reason, with or without cause. Employee and Employer can terminate the services “at-will” and will not liable to give any notice period. Employer will not make any reasonable effort to provide Employee with any written notice for the termination of employment except as follows: if Employee's employment is terminated for cause, then Employer may effectuate this termination immediately following Employer's determination of the facts or circumstances creating such cause, and this termination may be effectuated without any prior notice to Employee. In the event that you do not commence employment with Maven Workforce for any reason after having filed your H1B application, or leave the company within 24 months of having filed the H1B, you shall pay a sum of $10,000 as liquidated damages to Maven Workforce as reimbursement to cover recruiting, relocation, training costs and other related costs incurred on your behalf. The attorney fees will also be recovered, if incurred any, during the reimbursement process.

6. Expenses:

The employee shall be reimbursed for all ordinary, reasonable, and necessary expenses incident to his employment only if pre-approved by the Employer, upon presentation by the Employee of itemized original receipts in the form requested by the Employer.

7. Timesheet:

EMPLOYEE will send in the timesheets describing the work details approved from his/her immediate manager to NA@mavenworkforce.com on every Monday or latest by Tuesday for the previous week worked by the EMPLOYEE. Our payroll is Semi- Monthly.

8. Exclusivity:

During the term of this Agreement, EMPLOYEE’s described services shall be exclusive to EMPLOYER, and EMPLOYEE agrees that he/she will not, without the prior written consent of EMPLOYER, during such term, perform such services for or on behalf of any company, business or interest other than EMPLOYER.

9. Non-Competition:

9.1 For a period of two (2) year following the date of termination of this Agreement, for any reason by either party, EMPLOYEE will not directly or indirectly, or through a “Competitor” of the EMPLOYER, provide or attempt to provide (or advise others of the opportunity to provide), and/or sell or market any services to a “Client” of the EMPLOYER without the specific “Written Consent” of the EMPLOYER (as such terms within quotations are defined in Section 9.2 below).

9.2 For purposes of this Section 9, the following definitions will control:

  9.2.1 a “Competitor” of the EMPLOYER shall include any individual, partnership, joint venture, association, limited liability company or other entity, which is at the time engaged wholly or partly, in the business of computer systems analysis, research, consulting, documentation, design, programming, contract programming, testing, systems implementation or computer software consulting.

  9.2.2 a “Client” shall mean any entity with which the EMPLOYER is currently doing business or any entity with which the EMPLOYER has done business in the most recent past twelve (12) months, to whom the EMPLOYEE has been assigned to in the past, or about whom the EMPLOYEE has gained knowledge solely through his employment with EMPLOYER.

  9.2.3 “Written Consent” shall mean a signed statement executed by the CEO of the EMPLOYER, Vikram Wadhawan, or such other person who may, from time to time, be acting as CEO, specifically waiving Section 9.1 above.

10. Availability of EMPLOYEE:

The payment of compensation by EMPLOYER pursuant to this Agreement is conditioned upon EMPLOYEE being ready, willing, and able at all times to render services at the request and direction of EMPLOYER.

11. Assignment:

EMPLOYER shall have the right to assign this Agreement to any individual, entity or other person.

12. Restrictive Covenants:

As a direct inducement to EMPLOYER to employ or continue to employ him/her, EMPLOYEE agrees to the terms and conditions contained in Schedules A, B, C and D of this Agreement, which are incorporated by this reference.

13. Remedies:

In the event of a breach or threatened breach by the EMPLOYEE of any of the provisions of Section 9 or the Schedules listed in Section 12 of this Agreement, EMPLOYER, in addition to and not in limitation of any other rights, remedies, or damages available at law or in equity shall be entitled to a temporary and permanent injunction in order to prevent or to restrain any such breach by EMPLOYEE, or by EMPLOYEE’s partners, agents, representatives, servants, employers, employees and/or any and all persons acting for or on behalf of EMPLOYEE. EMPLOYER shall also be entitled to have EMPLOYEE pay EMPLOYER’s attorneys’ fees incurred in any action to enforce or defend any provision of this Agreement.

14. Accounting for Profits:

EMPLOYEE covenants and agrees that if he/she shall violate Sections 9 and/or 12 of this Agreement, EMPLOYER shall be entitled to an accounting and repayment of all profits, compensation, commissions, remuneration or benefits which EMPLOYEE shall receive out of or in connection with any such violation; such remedy shall be in addition to and not in limitation of any injunctive relief or other rights or remedies to which EMPLOYER is or may be entitled at law or in equity pursuant to this Agreement.

15. Reasonableness of Restrictions:

EMPLOYEE has carefully read and considered the provisions of this Agreement and having done so agrees that the restrictions set forth herein, including but not limited to the time period of such restrictions, are fair and reasonable and are reasonably required for the protection of EMPLOYER, its officers, directors and other employees, and will not unreasonably impair EMPLOYEE in his/her attempt to earn a livelihood.

16. Severability:

Each of the above provisions are independent and severable. Should any part of them be declared invalid or unenforceable by any court of competent jurisdiction, such decision shall not affect the validity of the remaining covenants, which shall remain in full force and effect. Should any such court determine that any provision of this Agreement is unenforceable; the parties agree that said provision shall be modified to the extent necessary to make it enforceable in the interpretation of such court. Said provision as modified and all the remaining terms of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.

17. Governing Law:

This agreement and it validity, performance and effect shall be determined and governed by the laws of New Jersey notwithstanding the fact that a party now or in the future may reside or be located outside of New Jersey and notwithstanding any conflict-of-law provisions to the contrary in the substantive law of the State of EMPLOYEE’S residence.

DISCLAIMER

I have entered into my employment relationship with EMPLOYER voluntarily and acknowledge the agreement in its entirety. I further understand that no agreement affecting my employment status shall be effective unless it is in writing and signed by me and by the EMPLOYER.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Agreement in duplicate as of the day and year first above written.

By: _______________________

Employee Name: {{ doc.item_name }} 		Date: _______________________
Maven Workforce INC, Employer

Signatures
By: Vikram Wadhawan Date: _______________________

SCHEDULE A – CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

EMPLOYEE acknowledges that, in and as a result of his/her employment with EMPLOYER, he/she will be exposed to, make use of, acquire and/or add to confidential information of a special and unique nature and value relating to such matters as EMPLOYER’s trade secrets, client lists, recruiting lists, contracts, systems, procedures, manuals, courseware, reports, methods used and preferred by EMPLOYER’s clients, fees paid by such clients, as well as other confidential and private information. EMPLOYEE covenants and agrees that:

1. During his/her term of employment, he/she will not make copies of any confidential information or materials for any non-employment related purposes.
2. Upon termination of his/her employment, he/she will turn over to EMPLOYER all originals and copies of any confidential written or otherwise recorded information or materials which he/she may have possession of.
3. He/she shall not, at any time during the term of his/her employment and for the period of two year following the term of his/her employment by EMPLOYER use or disclose, for any purpose whatsoever, any confidential information or materials which have been obtained by or disclosed to him/her as a result of or during his/her employment with EMPLOYER.

SCHEDULE B – RECRUITMENT

EMPLOYEE acknowledges that one of EMPLOYER’s most valuable assets is the employees, subcontractors, and potential employees of EMPLOYER. EMPLOYEE covenants and agrees that during the term of his/her employment by EMPLOYER and for the period of two year following the term of his/her employment by EMPLOYER, he/she will not offer, directly or indirectly, any employment and/or employment to, or assist, directly or indirectly, any other person or entity in offering any employment and/or employment to, any employee, EMPLOYEE, subcontractor, or prospective employee of EMPLOYER. “Prospective employee” shall mean any person to whom EMPLOYER has made attempts at recruitment for employment within two (2) year prior to EMPLOYEE’s termination of employment with EMPLOYER.

SCHEDULE C - MARKETING COMPETITION

EMPLOYEE acknowledges that during the term of his/her employment with EMPLOYER, he/she will learn valuable information in regard to EMPLOYER’s methods and procedures for selling consulting and training services to clients of EMPLOYER and will learn the identity of clients of EMPLOYER, all of which information is kept confidential by EMPLOYER. EMPLOYEE covenants and agrees that, for the period of two (2) year following the term of his/her employment by EMPLOYER, he/she will not sell or otherwise provide or market consulting or training services or assist, directly or indirectly, any other person or entity in selling, marketing or otherwise providing consulting or training services to any client or prospective client of EMPLOYER. “Clients” shall be limited to those clients of EMPLOYER for whom EMPLOYEE has provided consulting or training services and/or sold or marketed consulting or training services on behalf of EMPLOYER within the period of two (2) year prior to his/her termination of employment with EMPLOYER. A “prospective client” shall mean any person or entity to whom EMPLOYEE attempted to sell consulting or training services within the period of two (2) year prior to his/her termination of employment with EMPLOYER.

SCHEDULE D - CONSULTING & INSTRUCTION COMPETITION

EMPLOYEE acknowledges that during the term of his/her employment with EMPLOYER, he/she will receive valuable instruction relating to EMPLOYER’s methods and procedures for performing consulting and training services for clients of EMPLOYER and will learn the identity of clients of EMPLOYER, all of which information is kept confidential by EMPLOYER. EMPLOYEE covenants and agrees that, for the period of two (2) year following the term of his/her employment by EMPLOYER, he/she will not perform, directly or indirectly, consulting or training services or assist, directly or indirectly, any other person or entity in performing consulting or training services for any client of EMPLOYER for whom EMPLOYEE performed consulting or training services within the period of two (2) year prior to his/her termination of employment with EMPLOYER.

By: _______________________

Employee Name: {{ doc.item_name }} 		Date: _______________________
Maven Workforce INC, Employer

Signatures
By: Vikram Wadhawan Date: _______________________

CERTIFICATION

I certify, understand, agree and represent that:

    1. I have read and reviewed this document and have had the opportunity to have it reviewed by an attorney of my choice.


    2. The documents provided and foregoing statements made by me are, to the best of knowledge and belief, true and complete.


    3. Neither Maven Workforce nor any of its personnel has forcefully made me sign this agreement. No promises, inducements, or representations have been made to me except as stated in this Agreement and in the Date offer letter.


    4. I understand that, if I intentionally omit or provide false information on or in relation to this employment offer and agreement, then this employment offer and agreement may become null and void retroactively and I may face legal liability, including legal action based on the fraud, as per the laws of New Jersey.

By signing below, I acknowledge that I have read, reviewed and understand this document and I am signing of my own free will.
